statement.markdown: Implement a function that checks if two strings are anagrams of each other. An anagram is a word or phrase formed by rearranging the letters of a different word or phrase, typically using all the original letters exactly once.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be two strings of lowercase letters.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the strings are anagrams, false otherwise.
statement.notes: This is a classic problem in computer science. You can use any data structure or algorithm you like.

constraints.description: Input strings will not be longer than 10000 characters.

examples.explanation: The letters in the first string can be rearranged to spell the second string.
The first string has only three letters, but the second string has four.

hints.text: You can sort the characters in each string and then compare the sorted strings.

editorial.title: Anagram Checker
editorial.intuition: An anagram is just a rearrangement of the letters, so we can count the occurrences of each letter in each string and compare the results.
editorial.bruteForce: We can sort the characters in each string and then compare the sorted strings.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a frequency table to count the occurrences of each letter in each string.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a frequency table and a single loop to count the occurrences of each letter in each string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: If the two strings are anagrams, their frequency tables will be equal.
editorial.dryRun: Here's an example of how we can use a frequency table to solve this problem:
commonMistakes: Not considering the case of strings with different lengths.
